title: List image contents without running it
body:
Is there a way to list contents of an image downloaded from Docker Hub without running it?

I am looking for an alternative to `podman export` which works on images.

comments.body:
You can create a container and then mount it.

ctr=$(buildah from IMAGE)
mnt=$(buildah mount $ctr)
cd $mnt
and start looking at the image.
@rhatdan which means to run the container, right?

No the image is just mounted and you can go to the rootfs of the image and examine it with commands from the host.
For example
rpm -qa --root $mnt

To list all of the rpms on the system

find $mnt 

To list all of the files.

But you don't need to actually execute any code in the image.
Is it possible to work with the image without Linux kernel?

You could pull the image with skopeo as a tar ball, and then untar the layers.  
Containers/storage has some windows support also, so maybe you could pull an image onto Windows storage. 
Since I don't use non linux systems, I am not an expert in this.
I believe the questions have been answered, so I'm closing this PR.
I think frankly this should be reopened as the suggested method is too inconvenient and should not be accepted as-is. I think podman could easily provide a more developer-friendly alternative here.

@svenstaro [use `crane`](https://github.com/google/go-containerregistry/pull/1274).

```sh
brew install crane
crane export ubuntu - | tar -tvf -
```

Maybe `podman/buildah` can compile this `go` code in. I don't know.
Took me just 2.5 years of going without a job to scratch this itch. :D
Thanks but it's still a crutch. Podman should support it directly.

Podman does this too.

podman image mount
and
podman container mount
Huh didn't find that before. I guess that's good enough for me.
